methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
WB Group is a pre-IPO Defense & Security company founded by Piotr Wojciechowski, Adam Bartosiewicz, and Krzysztof Wysocki. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 10 companies—2 publicly traded (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private-stage peers including Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, Saronic, and Quantum-Systems—across the Defense & Security sector.

The peer-median method was not applicable due to insufficient stage-aligned comparables. While 19 Defense & Security peers exist in the dataset, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier companies yielded fewer than three datapoints in each category. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity of Defense & Security business models, margins, and growth profiles. This constraint limits triangulation to public market benchmarks and qualitative positioning against the identified private peer set.

Without sufficient narrow-stage comparables and absent disclosed financials for WB Group, a quantitative valuation range cannot be responsibly derived from the data provided. Any valuation exercise would require either (1) disclosure of WB Group's revenue, profitability, or growth metrics; (2) expanded dataset of pre-IPO Defense & Security peers at similar scale; or (3) application of precedent transaction analysis tied to specific deal structures. The key uncertainty remains the company's financial profile relative to the public and private peer cohort.
